title: bursera coyucensis
intro: Bursera coyucensis, also known as the Coyote Tree or Mexican Copal, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Burseraceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant has various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions, as well as having potential anti-cancer properties. Bursera coyucensis can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin.
